_header.file_role: E1 anchor fleet AC baseline config (hand-written, wave 1 scaffold)

metadata.source_note: Meta Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct: 80 dense decoder layers, d_model 8192, GQA (64 Q heads / 8 KV heads, d_head 128), SwiGLU FFN 28672, vocab 128256, RoPE base 500000, RMSNorm eps 1e-5, untied embeddings. Architecture facts: meta-llama/llama-models models/sku_list.py llama3_3_70b_instruct arch_args, which are byte-identical to the llama3_1_70b entry (3.3 is a post-training refresh of the 3.1 70B architecture); FFN 28672 derived via the official formula in models/llama3/model.py (4*dim -> *2/3 -> *1.3 -> round up to multiple_of 4096); cross-checked against the Llama 3 herd paper (arXiv 2407.21783v3, Table 3: 80 layers / 8192 dim / 28672 FFN / 64 heads / 8 KV heads) and the Llama 3.3 model card (70B, GQA, 128k context, 15T+ tokens). Only the Instruct variant was released, so the serving anchor uses meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct. Unrepresented in schema 0.3: llama3-type RoPE scaling (factor 8, 8k -> 128k) encoded as none with original_max_position 8192; E1 anchor workloads run at <= 2560 tokens so predictions are unaffected. params counts follow AC's parameter ledger (2*vocab*d embeddings + 2*d_model RMSNorm per layer); for dense models the ledger books active == total, matching configs/mistral_7b.json conventions.
